questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: a. Some books are bulbs. b. All biscuits are pens. c. No watch is a biscuit. Conclusions: I. Some books are not biscuits. II. Some bulbs are not pens. III. Some books are not pens. IV. Some pens are not books.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: a. All books are notes. b. Some notes are watches. c. No watch is a pencil. Conclusions: I. Some watches are books. II. Some notes are pencils. III. No watch is a book. IV. Some papers are not pencils.

choices.a: I and either II or IV follow
choices.e: Either I or III and IV follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: a. Some professors are Indians. b. Some Asians are blondes. c. No Asian is a brunette. Conclusions: I. Some Indians are Asians. II. Some blondes are brunettes. III. Some brunettes are not blondes. IV. Some blondes are not brunettes.

choices.e: Either II or IV and III follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: a. All rips are tips. b. No coin is a tip. c. Some balls are rips. Conclusions: I. Some balls are coins. II. Some balls are tips. III. Some coins are not balls. IV. Some balls are not coins.

choices.e: Either I or IV, and III follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: a. All crooks are spooks. b. All books are jokes. c. Some books are crooks. Conclusions: I. Some jokes are crooks. II. Some spooks are jokes. III. Some crooks are jokes. IV. Some spooks are books.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: a. Some papers are nibs. b. No file is a cutter. c. Some files are papers. Conclusions: I. Some files are nibs. II. Some papers are cutters. III. Some files are not nibs. IV. Some papers are not cutters.

choices.a: Either I or III, and IV follow
choices.b: Either I or III, and IV follow
choices.c: Either II or IV, and III follow
choices.d: Either I or IV, and either I or III follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: a. All gardens are boxes. b. Some gardens are flowers. c. No chocolate is a box. Conclusions: I. Some flowers are chocolates. II. No garden is a chocolate. III. Some flowers are boxes. IV. Some flowers are not chocolates.

choices.c: Only III and either I or IV follow
choices.d: Only II, III and either I or IV follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: a. Some bulbs are chocolates. b. No fruit is a bulb. c. Only Amul is a chocolate. Conclusions: I. Some fruits are not chocolates. II. Some chocolates are not fruits. III. Some bulbs are Amuls. IV. No bulb is an Amul.

choices.d: Either III or IV and I follow
